title: Daily summary reports off by a day for west-of-UTC merchants
product_summary: Acme Reports - daily merchant summary emails (Python, stdlib only)

ground_truth.root_cause: generate_daily_report() in acme_reports/reports.py computes the report date and day boundaries from the UTC calendar date (now.date() plus a UTC-midnight window) while each account has an IANA timezone; for accounts west of UTC the evening report covers the wrong day (labeled tomorrow's date, missing the day's orders), and east-of-UTC accounts drift the other way. Boundaries must be the account-local day converted to UTC (zoneinfo).
ground_truth.fix_contract: With an injected clock and an account timezone, the report must cover the account's local calendar day: for an America/Los_Angeles account at 2026-03-10T01:30Z the report is dated 2026-03-09 and contains exactly the orders placed within the LA-local Mar 9 (including ones with UTC timestamps on Mar 10), and for an Asia/Tokyo account at 2026-03-09T22:00Z the report is dated 2026-03-10 with the Tokyo-local day's orders. UTC accounts keep their current behavior.
expected_evidence: report_generated events with order_count 0 concentrated on accounts whose account_timezone is west of UTC (e.g. America/Los_Angeles), while the same merchants clearly have orders
report_generated report_date values one day ahead of the account-local date at generation time
distractors: data pipeline sync timing blamed by the customer (orders are already committed when the report runs)
email delivery delay / send scheduling (the report content itself is wrong, not late)
the emailer template in acme_reports/emailer.py (renders whatever the report contains)
